title: Constants, Literals, Domain Types, and Configuration · Failure Case: Units Configuration
summary: Replace ambiguous magic values without creating meaningless constant noise. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates units configuration increment in a domain constant and configuration policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate units, deploy-time configuration, feature flags, and code constants.
Use enums, unions, literal types, branded values, or value objects for closed domains.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
